Source title:
IVCSD elects new leadership, discusses plans for Deltopia alternative

Facts:
* The Isla Vista Community Services District discussed plans for an alternative event to Deltopia after Santa Barbara County adopted a noise ordinance that effectively canceled the unsanctioned street festival.
* The proposed alternative event, working-named Novatopia, would include live music, art, markets, beer gardens, bathrooms, safety resources and multiple entertainment zones across downtown Isla Vista.
* IVCSD staff discussed wristbands for Isla Vista residents, event-lead applications, permits, safety planning, sponsorships and a budget above $250,000.
* One community member argued that if IVCSD hosts a festival on Deltopia weekend, it would effectively become Deltopia itself.
* The board also addressed parking compliance software and broader community governance issues at the same meeting.

Local tension:
Officials are trying to replace an unofficial student party tradition with a planned, permitted, safety-oriented event, while some community members worry the alternative may reproduce similar crowd and party dynamics.

Source title:
Bear sightings reported at UCSB, Goleta

Facts:
* UCPD reported a black bear sighting near Pollock Theater and the Intercollegiate Athletics building early in the morning.
* UCPD sent an emergency alert asking students to avoid the area, then later lifted the request after an extensive search did not locate the bear.
* Other bear sightings were reported around Goleta and near Santa Ynez Apartments during the same week.
* UCPD said the California Department of Fish and Wildlife was notified and urged students and community members to be aware of their surroundings and report sightings.
* Campus safety guidance told people to make themselves appear large, make loud noises, avoid attracting smells and not feed the bear.

Local tension:
A campus usually organized around classes, alerts and student routines briefly had to treat wildlife behavior as a public-safety issue, creating a mismatch between bureaucratic emergency messaging and the absurd image of a bear wandering through familiar UCSB spaces.

Source title:
‘Jesus people cooking Jesus Burgers’: a look into Del Playa’s 20-year tradition

Facts:
* Since 2001, Jesus Burgers has given out free burgers on Friday nights at 6686 Del Playa Drive in Isla Vista.
* The group estimates it has served over 200,000 burgers and now gives out around 200 to 250 burgers per Friday night during the school year.
* Jesus Burgers is connected to Isla Vista Mission and other Christian groups, and volunteers often offer free blessings or conversations about faith.
* The Friday-night crowd includes intoxicated and hungry partygoers on Del Playa, creating a mix of party culture, religious outreach and late-night food demand.
* Residents and volunteers describe the effort as a way to serve and care for Isla Vista, while also acknowledging that some students joke about it, misunderstand it or treat it as strange.

Local tension:
A religious outreach project has become part of Isla Vista’s Friday-night party ecosystem, where free burgers, blessings, intoxicated students and community care all coexist in the same Del Playa ritual.

prompt:
Write one Onion-style local satire headline based on the source below.

Source title:
IVCSD implements I.V. parking compliance program

Facts:
* The Isla Vista Community Services District planned to implement a parking compliance program in November to address longstanding parking issues in Isla Vista.
* The program aims to address health and safety concerns, mobility issues for pedestrians and disabled individuals and greenhouse gas emissions by enforcing statewide parking regulations.
* Several Isla Vista streets regularly have 60-100% parking occupancy, making parking a persistent concern for residents, students and visitors.
* IVCSD planned to introduce Tina the Toad, a Gen Z-oriented parking compliance mascot, to educate the community about parking laws in a non-threatening way through tabling, banners, giveaways and ticket-like guideline slips on cars.
* The program would eventually test ticketing for visible noncompliance, move toward a zero-tolerance policy in April and need roughly one ticket per hour to break even financially.

Local tension:
Isla Vista has a real parking shortage and safety problem, but the public-facing solution combines enforcement, revenue-neutral ticketing and a friendly youth-branded mascot meant to make parking rules feel less threatening.

* clearly grounded in the source issue
* specific to UCSB, Isla Vista, or Santa Barbara when appropriate
* clear satirical target
* avoid random absurdity
* avoid inventing facts not implied by the source
* output only one headline.

Source title:
Dominic Fike announced to headline Extravaganza 2026

Facts:
* UCSB's Associated Students Program Board announced the Extravaganza 2026 lineup at Storke Lawn on May 7.
* The lineup featured Dominic Fike as headliner, along with JT, Earl Sweatshirt and Jane Remover.
* Extravaganza is UCSB's annual student-only music festival and was scheduled for May 17 at Harder Stadium.
* The reveal used a 'Wild Wild X' western aesthetic, though an ASPB coordinator clarified that the theme was about the event's visual rollout rather than a clue about the artists.
* Initial student reactions were mostly positive, especially compared with mixed reactions to the previous year's lineup.
* Some students were excited by the major artists, while others said they were less familiar with parts of the lineup or compared it to past headliners like Playboi Carti and Steve Aoki.
* An ASPB coordinator described how negative social media reactions to last year's Extravaganza affected the organizing team, while emphasizing that supportive responses can be quieter than criticism.

Local tension:
A student-run campus festival has to choose a lineup broad enough to satisfy a fragmented undergraduate audience, while organizers face immediate public judgment through campus social media and comparisons to past headliners.
